home.title: PostGuard
home.par: PostGuard offers free and easy-to-use end-to-end-encryption for emails and files. Only the intended recipients can decrypt and read the emails and files you send with PostGuard.
home.subpar1: Encrypted email isn't new. But PGP and S/MIME are so hard to use that, in one study, only 1 in 10 pairs of people managed to exchange a single encrypted message. PostGuard was built so encryption is something anyone can actually use.

landing.heroHeadline: Send encrypted email and files. No keys, no setup.
landing.heroSubtext: Traditional email encryption makes everyone swap keys before the first message, and most people never manage it. PostGuard encrypts to a person's identity instead: send right away, and your recipient proves who they are with the Yivi app to open it.

howItWorks.step1Desc: Drag in the files you want to share. Up to 5 GB, encrypted right in your browser.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Just their email address, no keys to exchange. Optionally add their date of birth or phone number.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Confirm with the Yivi app, and your files are on their way.

landing.feature1Desc: No generating, exchanging or safeguarding public and private keys. PostGuard encrypts to your recipient's identity, and they just prove who they are with the Yivi app.
landing.feature2Desc: You don't need anything from your recipient in advance. Send now; they verify their identity afterwards to read it, with no accounts or installs beforehand.
landing.feature3Desc: Files are encrypted in your browser, so only the intended recipient can open them. Free and open source, developed at Radboud University.
landing.businessDesc: Send encrypted emails and files programmatically with the PostGuard API, or use your own email domain. Built for organisations that need secure communication at scale.
landing.businessCardDesc: Encrypt emails and files programmatically with the API, or send from your own domain. Built for organisations that need secure communication at scale.
landing.business1Desc: Integrate encrypted email and file sharing directly into your applications using API keys. Send end-to-end encrypted messages programmatically.
landing.business2Desc: Send encrypted emails from your own domain. Recipients see your brand, while PostGuard handles the encryption behind the scenes.
landing.business3Desc: Run PostGuard on your own infrastructure for full control over your data, encryption keys, and compliance requirements.
landing.business4Desc: Revoke access to sent emails at any time and track when recipients have decrypted your messages.
landing.business5Desc: Brand the encryption experience as your own and maintain a complete audit trail of all encrypted communications.
landing.business6Title: Bring your own storage & DNS verification
landing.business6Desc: Store encrypted files on your own infrastructure and verify your domain with DNS records, similar to Mailchimp's domain authentication.

limits.sectionTitle: Send large files. Without limits that get in the way.
limits.intro: Most encrypted file-sharing services cap free transfers at just a few hundred megabytes. PostGuard lets you share much more, completely free.
limits.uploadDesc: Send a single file or a whole batch, up to 5 GB in one encrypted transfer.
limits.rollingDesc: Up to 5 GB of encrypted file sharing per email address, over a rolling 14-day window.
limits.footnote: No account. No payment. End-to-end encrypted, always.

about.title: About PostGuard
about.simple.title: In short
about.simple.body:
New here? This is PostGuard in plain language. The full details are below.

  • PostGuard locks your email and files so only the person you choose can open them.
  • You send it, and only the right person can unlock it. No one in between can read it.
  • To open a message, the receiver proves who they are with the free Yivi app on their phone.
  • The receiver only shares what is needed, like an email address. Nothing more.
  • PostGuard is free and is made to keep your messages private.
about.subpar1: PostGuard offers end-to-end encryption (E2EE) to increase the security of email and file sharing. Before sending an email or sharing a file, the email or file is encrypted. This means that the email or file is transformed in such a way that only authorized parties can decrypt it. Only the intended recipients can decrypt the email or file using a special secret key. “End-to-end”-part of E2EE means that the files and emails are encrypted in transit, from sender to receiver.
about.subpar2: PostGuard uses Identity-Based Encryption (IBE), which is a type of encryption that is easier to use than traditional public-key encryption. In particular, it addresses two problems: key management and recipient authentication. In traditional public-key encryption, each recipient has her own public key, which needs to be located and authenticated by the sender before it can be used to encrypt. In identity-based encryption, the public keys are generated directly from the identity of the recipient. The sender only needs to locate one master public key, and can encrypt any messages under any recipient's identity. To decrypt, the recipient then needs to authenticate herself to a trusted third party, which moves away the responsibility of authenticating the recipient from the sender.
about.subtitle3: What is Yivi and why does PostGuard use it?
about.subpar3: To decrypt and read encrypted emails and files, recipients only need to prove that they really are the intended recipient. This is done with the authentication app Yivi. PostGuard uses Yivi because it is a free, easy-to-use and privacy-friendly authentication app. Moreover, Yivi offers attribute-based authentication. This means that recipients are able to selectively disclose specific attributes (like having an email address, phone number or surname) of an email or file.
about.team.par: PostGuard was originally developed by a multidisciplinary team from iHub at Radboud University, in the Netherlands, funded by NWO. The aim is to enable secure communication by offering free email encryption based on advanced (identity-based) encryption techniques. The team focuses on usability and accessibility of encryption technology by combining expertise in computing science, security, and user experience design. PostGuard is currently being developed by Yivi at the Caesar Groep. This project partners with several public and private organisations in the Netherlands, including Nedap Healthcare, VGZ, SURFnet, the Municipality of Nijmegen and Compumatica. For questions or other inquiries, please contact us via our contact address.

addons.title: Add PostGuard to your mail client
addons.par: Using the PostGuard addon for Thunderbird or Outlook, you can send encrypted emails in a breeze!
addons.Thunderbird:
1. Download and save the file postguard-tb-addon.xpi somewhere on your computer, where you can find it again easily.

  2. Within Thunderbird:

    • under the menu Tools, go to Add-ons and Themes,
    • click on the cog icon next to Manage Your Extensions and select Install Add-on From File ...,
    • a file selection window opens; go to the folder where you stored the file postguard-tb-addon.xpi and select it,
    • Thunderbird tells you what an add-on can do; when you wish to continue, click the Add button.
  3. Now, when you compose a new mail message, a new header is shown with a button to switch PostGuard on or off. When on, it will encrypt the message for all recipients. Each recipient needs to use the Yivi app to prove that (s)he really posesses the email address that you used.
addons.Outlook:
1. Adding the add-on:

    • Note: PostGuard for Outlook only works in New Outlook and Outlook on the web. Classic Outlook is not supported.
    • Download the manifest.xml file and save it somewhere you can find it again.
    • Open aka.ms/olksideload in your browser.
    • Click My add-ins.
    • Under Custom Add-ins, click Add a custom add-in, then Add from file.... If you do not see this option, your organization probably does not allow custom add-ins to be installed. Please contact your administrator.
    • Select the manifest.xml file you just downloaded and confirm to install.
    • Restart Outlook (close and reopen the desktop client, or refresh the browser tab if you are using Outlook on the web) to complete the activation of the add-in.
  2. Now, when you compose a new mail message, the PostGuard add-in is available from the Apps menu in the Outlook ribbon. Note: Outlook currently lists custom add-ins under the Apps menu rather than as a dedicated ribbon button — click Apps in the ribbon, then select PostGuard Add-in to open it. The add-in contains a button called Send encrypted Email. When clicking, it will encrypt and send the message to all recipients. Each recipient needs to use the Yivi app to prove that (s)he really possesses the email address that you used. Also, a copy of the sent mail is stored in the folder PostGuard Sent Items as a draft email (currently, Microsoft only allows to create messages as a draft).
  3. After receiving a PostGuard encrypted email, the email can be decrypted by opening the message, clicking Apps in the Outlook ribbon, selecting PostGuard Add-in, and then clicking the Decrypt Email button.

  4. What to expect when sending or decrypting:

    • Security prompts are normal. When you send or decrypt a message, Outlook opens a small PostGuard wants to open a dialog window. This is a standard Outlook security prompt for any add-in that opens a dialog — click Allow (or OK) to continue. The Yivi signing prompt that follows is also part of the standard process; it is how PostGuard verifies your identity before encrypting or decrypting.
    • If nothing happens after clicking the button, your browser or Outlook is likely blocking pop-ups for the add-in dialog. Allow pop-ups for outlook.office.com (web) or for the PostGuard add-in domain (desktop), then try again. In most browsers you can do this from the pop-up icon that appears in the address bar after the click is blocked.
addons.outlookWarning: Heads up: some Outlook accounts (typically those provided by an organization) do not allow custom add-ins. If your account is one of them, the Custom Add-ins section described below will be missing from aka.ms/olksideload and PostGuard for Outlook cannot be installed. Please contact your administrator, or use PostGuard for Thunderbird or the web app instead.

fallback.extensionPrompt: Want to decrypt emails directly in Thunderbird or Outlook?

welcome.title: Welcome to your Inbox
welcome.description: This is your personal space to read encrypted PostGuard emails. Everything happens right here in your browser. Your emails are never sent to a server.
welcome.step1: Upload the "postguard.encrypted" file you received as an email attachment
welcome.step2: Verify your identity with the Yivi app
welcome.step3: Read your decrypted email. It's saved here for next time
welcome.privacy: Your emails are stored only in your browser. Clear them anytime in settings.

decrypt.helper: Decrypt this mail by showing who you are with Yivi

delete.emails: Are you sure you want to delete all emails? This action is permanent!
delete.creds: Are you sure you want to delete all personal data? This action is permanent!

list.nothing2: Upload a "postguard.encrypted" file above to decrypt and read your first email.

filesharing.title: File Sharing
filesharing.subpar1: With PostGuard you can securely send files in a privacy-friendly manner. Every file is encrypted specifically for the receiver. Only that person can decrypt that file. To do that, the email address must be shown. For this, the identification app Yivi is used. Learn more about the Yivi-app.
filesharing.error: Oops... We are really sorry but something went wrong. Please try again or contact us
filesharing.serverError: Our server encountered an error while processing your request. Please try again later. If the problem persists, please contact us.

crash.title: Something went wrong
crash.message: An unexpected error broke this part of the page. You can try again, or send the error details to PostGuard so we can fix it.
crash.failed: Could not reach the reporting server. Please try again later.

sign.scanQR: Verify your identity by scanning this QR code with the free Yivi app on your phone.
sign.signOtherDevice: Sign with Yivi on another device
sign.followSteps: Tap the button below to open the Yivi app and verify your identity.
sign.oneTapWaiting: Complete the steps in the Yivi app, then return here.

decryptpanel.pageDescription: To download the files, you need to decrypt them. You do this by proving who you are with Yivi.
decryptpanel.irmaInstructionQr: Decrypt the files by verifying your e-mail address. Scan the QR code below with the free Yivi app on your phone.
decryptpanel.irmaInstructionMobile: Decrypt the files by verifying your e-mail address. Please click the button below to open the Yivi-app.
decryptpanel.askDownloadText: Press the button below to start decrypting and downloading your files
decryptpanel.doneMessage: Your files are being downloaded and decrypted
decryptpanel.doneMessageComplete: Your files have been downloaded and decrypted
decryptpanel.readyToDownload: Your files have been decrypted and are ready to download
decryptpanel.trustWarnEmailOnly: The sender only verified an email address. Anyone with access to that mailbox could have signed these files. Check that the address truly belongs to who you expect, ideally via a channel other than email, before downloading.
decryptpanel.trustWarnUnsigned: This file is not signed. There is no verified sender at all, so anyone could have created it and there is no way to confirm where it came from. Only continue if you are certain you trust the source.
decryptpanel.discardedBody: The files were not downloaded. If you want them after all, ask the sender to share again.
decryptpanel.notFoundSubtitle: The link may have expired, or there may be an error in it.
decryptpanel.notFoundMessage: Ask the sender to send the files once more.
decryptpanel.serverErrorSubtitle: Our server encountered an error while processing your request.
decryptpanel.serverErrorMessage: Please try again later. If the problem persists, please contact us.
decryptpanel.identityMismatchSubtitle: The identity you provided does not match the intended recipient.
decryptpanel.identityMismatchMessage: Please make sure you are proving the correct email address in Yivi.
decryptpanel.sessionExpiredSubtitle: The server no longer has this upload available.
decryptpanel.sessionExpiredMessage: Ask the sender to send the files once more.

encryptPanel.RecipientsHelpToggle: What should I fill in here?
encryptPanel.RecipientsText: Specify the recipients and what attributes they must prove to decrypt the files. Only the recipients will see this data.
encryptPanel.attributesExplainer: Optionally require the recipient to prove more about themselves, like their date of birth or phone number, before they can open the files.
encryptPanel.emailSenderSubHeadingToggle: Why do you need this information?
encryptPanel.emailSenderSubHeading: Let the recipient(s) know these files are from you. Before sending, you sign the files with the Yivi app by proving your email address. You can optionally also share your name from a municipality, passport, ID card, or driving licence.
encryptPanel.messageText: This message will not be encrypted and will be included in the notification email.
encryptPanel.yiviInfoText: Yivi is a free and privacy-friendly authentication app. With Yivi you can prove who you are by selectively sharing personal data, such as your email address, phone number, or name. At PostGuard we use Yivi to securely encrypt and decrypt files.
encryptPanel.yiviTip: Tip: In the Yivi app you can also add an optional phone number or date of birth. This way you let the recipient(s) know for sure that these files come from you.
encryptPanel.encryptingInfo: Your files are being encrypted first and afterwards are sent to:
encryptPanel.succesInfo: Your encrypted files have been encrypted and uploaded. An email with a download link has been sent to:
encryptPanel.senderConfirmation: A confirmation email has also been sent to you.
encryptPanel.whatRecipientGetsText: The recipient receives an email with a link to the PostGuard website. On this page, they can download the files by proving their identity with the Yivi app. They scan a QR code with Yivi, after which they gain access to the encrypted files and can decrypt them.
encryptPanel.irmaInstructionHeaderQr: Show who you are by scanning the QR-code with Yivi
encryptPanel.irmaInstructionQr: Encrypt the files by verifying your e-mail address. Please scan the QR-code below with the identification app Yivi.
encryptPanel.irmaInstructionMobile: Encrypt the files by verifying your e-mail address. Please click the button below to open the Yivi-app.

privacypolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacypolicy.simple.title: In short
privacypolicy.simple.body:
This is a short summary in plain language. The full privacy policy is below.

  • You can use PostGuard in two ways: send an encrypted email yourself with the add-on, or share files through the PostGuard website.
  • The person you send to opens the message by proving who they are with the free Yivi app.
  • With the email add-on, your email is encrypted on your own device and sent through your own email account, so it does not go through PostGuard. The only exception: if the email is too large to send, the encrypted email is uploaded to our file server.
  • With the PostGuard website, you upload your files to us, and we email the recipient (and you, for confirmation) to say that files are waiting. We keep the encrypted files for at most 2 weeks, then delete them. We cannot read them.
  • We only ever need an email address. You can choose to add more, like your name, phone number, or date of birth. This can show the recipient who you are, or make sure that only the right person can open the message. It is always your choice and never required.
  • PostGuard is free. It is run by Yivi at the Caesar Groep, with ProcoliX for file storage. You can contact us, or complain to the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens).
privacypolicy.full:
PostGuard is a free platform for securely sending emails and files. These are two separate services. They both offer confidentiality: protection against reading by the wrong person(s). PostGuard encrypts your emails and files in such a way that only the recipient that you choose can decrypt, and then read them. This works via the free Yivi app for attribute-based authentication: you, as sender choose certain personal attributes of the intended receiver (such as email address, phone number or name). The receiver must then prove, via the Yivi app, to be in possession of these attributes, before the mail/file can be decrypted and read. This is the essence of PostGuard: it reduces confidentiality to authentication. PostGuard is offered for free, as a social service, to make encrypted mail and file sharing easy for everyone. PostGuard was originally developed by the iHub team at Radboud University (RU), with support from NWO and SIDN fonds.

PostGuard is now operated by Yivi in the Netherlands. As explained in more detail below, Yivi is the data controller, in the sense of Europe's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): it determines the purposes and means. Yivi is also a PostGuard data processor. For PostGuard file sharing Yivi is a data processor jointly with the company ProcoliX. Only data that is necessary for the PostGuard service is processed.

Both email and file encryption make use of Identity-Based-Encryption (IBE) via a Key Generation Service (KGS) that is run by Yivi. A receiver of an email or file can obtain a decryption key from the KGS after proper authentication (via Yivi). The disclosed attributes are processed very briefly by Yivi in order to check if they match the requirements, so that a decryption key can be generated. Immediately after key generation, or after a failed disclosure, the relevant attributes (and associated timestamped IP-addresses) are deleted. Yivi does not maintain a log of these data in such disclosures. Yivi does count the number of disclosures and the rough location (at country level) of their origin. These data are used only for usage statistics and service improvements.

  • When you use PostGuard to send encrypted emails, these emails are encrypted on your own device and follow their usual routes via your own email provider. They do not pass through Yivi, either as plaintext or encrypted. Hence Yivi does not process your emails. The only exception is when an email is too large to send directly by email: in that case the encrypted email is uploaded to the file-sharing server (hosted by ProcoliX), just like the file transfer described below. If you use the PostGuard browser-based decryption service for decrypting your emails, the actual decryption takes place in your own web browser. Hence, also in this case, Yivi does not process your emails.
  • Transferring files via PostGuard happens via a website hosted by ProcoliX. Via this website, only the email addresses of the sender and the recipient are processed. These are needed to send notifications about the transfer, both to the recipient and to you as a confirmation. The receiver's email address is also used as a (standard) attribute to enable encryption and decryption via PostGuard. Only the email address is mandatory: as the sender, you may optionally require the recipient to prove extra attributes (such as their name, phone number, or date of birth), and you may optionally sign the transfer with your own verified attributes (such as your name, phone number, or date of birth). These extras are never required. Sent emails and email addresses are not stored longer than strictly necessary, that is, at most 2 weeks. When you use PostGuard to transfer files, the encrypted files will be stored temporarily at ProcoliX. ProcoliX cannot see the contents of these files because they are encrypted. Each file is accompanied by some basic metadata, like the type of attribute used for encryption and a timestamp. The attribute values are purged. The encrypted files plus these metadata are kept for a maximum of 2 weeks and are then automatically deleted. Other technical personal data, such as IP addresses, are stored and deleted according to ProcoliX's general policy. When everything is running normally, this data is deleted after two days. ProcoliX temporarily stores the encrypted files but does not have access to the cryptographic keys (private keys) required for decryption. Yivi generates the private keys but does not have access to the encrypted files. ProcoliX and Yivi do not conspire to jointly decrypt files behind the backs of users.

Technical changes to the PostGuard system, or any new services, may result in modification of this privacy policy. Yivi and ProcoliX reserve the right to make such changes and will announce the new privacy policy as soon as possible via this page. For any questions, comments, or complaints about this processing by Yivi and/or ProcoliX for PostGuard, please contact the PostGuard team at our contact address. For complaints about the processing of data by Yivi or ProcoliX, the Dutch Authority for the Protection of Personal Data (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) can also be contacted.
